inputs.path: founder-authored Outcome First Engineering proposal (in-session, not repo-tracked)

stride.control_or_reason: author strings render as plain Markdown body / inside ~~~text fences in any generated security artifact; never select control fields (tm-/oc-/cuj- ids, resolution verbs, gate outcomes)
author may paste a real customer name / secret into an outcome scenario or journey; routed to /slo-verify Pass 4 PII scan + data-classification row
authored outcome text is descriptive only; it cannot choose a tm-/oc-/cuj- id, a resolution verb, or a gate outcome
Evidence Log records command/expected/actual per outcome row (who/what/when)
a green Pass 0 is 'proved at runtime for the declared rows', never 'whole system proved'
bounded to the milestone's declared journeys/regression rows
fail-closed: unknown/blank resolution treated as not-passing, never silently done
SHA baseline change is explicit and PR-reviewed; never waived

abuse_cases.surface: authored §5C/§17 strings interpolated into a generated SECURITY/threat-model artifact
abuse_cases.attack_step: Paste a crafted string (Markdown/YAML/HTML metacharacters or agent-readable instructions) into an Outcome statement, journey step, or scenario Then clause
abuse_cases.attacker_outcome: Template-placeholder injection: break out of the section or smuggle instructions into a generated security default
abuse_cases.control: Reuse the /slo-architect fence rule — every user-provided string in a generated security/threat artifact wrapped in ~~~text; outcome sections render author text as plain Markdown body only and never select control fields

abuse_cases.surface: /slo-execute (or any agent) authoring Outcome Scenarios / Critical User Journey tests
abuse_cases.attacker: An agent or hurried author optimising for green over truth
abuse_cases.attack_step: Emit an Outcome test that asserts something trivially true (assert(true), a 200 without the user outcome, or a mock's return instead of the real front-to-end path), then mark the Outcome Scenario passed
abuse_cases.attacker_outcome: A milestone closes as 'outcome proven' while the promised user value does not exist — the central AI-assisted-development failure mode
abuse_cases.control: §5C Front-to-End Validation requires PER-LAYER applicable|not_applicable(reason) steps (seed->backend->persisted record->API/IPC->UI) with >=1 real cross-layer assertion (e.g. backend->persisted) even when UI is N/A; /slo-critique flags vacuous/single-And/mock-only scenarios as ask; /slo-verify Pass 0 runs the journey front-to-end at runtime over the highest APPLICABLE layer chain (Playwright for UI), never a single mock; Outcome Scenarios require >=1 observable-outcome Then plus follow-on Ands per §11.3

abuse_cases.surface: /slo-retro outcome refusal gate + Core Capability Regression Matrix resolution column
abuse_cases.attack_step: Mark every Regression Matrix row pass (or waived_with_reason with an empty reason) without running the journeys, or set an Outcome Scenario passed without an Evidence Log row
abuse_cases.attacker_outcome: False 'nothing broke' / 'outcome exists' signal; an adjacent capability silently regresses
abuse_cases.control: Every matrix row resolves to exactly one of pass | not_applicable | waived_with_reason with a non-empty evidence path/reason (never blank, mirrors §5B Bundle discipline); /slo-verify Pass 0 re-runs the matrix journeys and writes actuals; /slo-retro refuses on any blank actual or reasonless waiver

abuse_cases.surface: the two existing SHA-pin test files (sap_imp_m5_agents.rs, slo_tm_m2_consumers.rs) allow-listed in M4
abuse_cases.attacker: An executor or contributor optimising for a green gate
abuse_cases.attack_step: Instead of updating only the slo-critique SHA baseline CONSTANT, weaken or remove the F-ENG-6 SHA assertion (or the ## Rotation order / persona-anchor assertions) in a pin test to make it pass
abuse_cases.attacker_outcome: The canonical-critique-path tamper-evidence is silently removed; slo-critique/SKILL.md can thereafter be modified undetected
abuse_cases.control: M4 constrains edits in those two files to the baseline-constant VALUE only (Forbidden shortcuts + guard); outcome_first_m4_consumers.rs cross-checks BOTH critique baselines == the live slo-critique SHA; PR review of the two pin files

residual_risks.risk: An author can still hand-author a thin/dishonest outcome contract or fake an Evidence-Log actual
residual_risks.exploit_path: The framework forces shape + re-runs journeys but cannot enforce author integrity; a determined author can fake a thin contract
residual_risks.compensating_control: /slo-verify Pass 0 runtime re-run + /slo-critique theatre review + /slo-retro refusal raise the cost of faking; mitigation not elimination (tm-outcome-first-abuse-2/3)

residual_risks.risk: This change ships discipline, not the target product's outcome-test runner
residual_risks.exploit_path: SLO defines the §5C contract + Pass 0 expectation; a target product with no E2E/Playwright harness can mark UI outcome rows not_applicable
residual_risks.compensating_control: The contract makes the front-to-end path the path of least resistance; not_applicable requires a reason; cross-device tooling explicitly deferred

residual_risks.risk: PII/secret pasted into an outcome scenario or journey could reach a git-tracked artifact
residual_risks.exploit_path: Author embeds a real customer name/credential in a §17 row that lands under a tracked path
residual_risks.compensating_control: Data-classification row + /slo-verify Pass 4 PII scan + ~~~text fence in generated artifacts; pii_scan_override + reason required for intentional matches

compliance.control: Outcome-test authority inversion = explicit, evidenced Definition of Done
compliance.soc2: CC8.1 change management — evidence before done

compliance.control: Security-BDD outcome rows exercise authn/authz + audit-event assertions
compliance.soc2: CC6.1 logical access; CC7.2 audit logging
compliance.asvs: V1 Architecture; V4 Access Control; V7 Logging

compliance.control: Reliability-BDD outcome rows (degraded/outage visible, no silent failure)
